result.lens: Equivariant topology of configuration spaces: monodromy of the finite 5-coloring fiber over the unit-realization variety, with twists supplied by SO(2)-symmetric embeddings (carousel holonomy)
result.first_principles: Fix a finite graph G and its unit-realization variety R(G) subset (R^2)^V modulo SE(2). Proper 5-colorings Col_5(G) form a finite fiber over R(G), and on the open stratum (no extra coincidences) this "coloring sheaf" is the TRIVIAL local system: the graph does not change as you flex. This single observation explains, from first principles, the repo's negative wall: all fiberwise (gadget-internal, port-local) structure is confined to the primitive monoid (L55), and exhaustive search finds every nonadjacent pair free (L56/L57), because locally there is nothing to find. Therefore any genuinely new forcing must come from the BASE: the topology of R(G) and its quotients. Exactly two sources of nontrivial monodromy exist. (a) Symmetry twists: a path in R(G) from p to p compose sigma (sigma in Aut(G)) is a loop in R(G)/Aut(G); its holonomy acts on Col_5(G) by c maps to c compose sigma. (b) Wall-crossing: at isolated real parameter values a flex creates extra unit coincidences (distance hits 1) or vertex coincidences (distance hits 0), changing the fiber by added constraints. Both sources are Euclidean-specific, and the three detectors confirm this is where the R^2-content lives. Over Q^2 there are no unit equilateral triangles, hence no chi-5 cores at all, and Q^2-points of realization varieties are totally disconnected: no flexes, no walls, no IVT. In L^infty the linear isometry group is the finite dihedral group (no rotation continuum to tune coincidences), unit circles meet in segments (no cocircular pinning, frameworks never rigid), and no chi-5 core embeds since chi = 4. On R^1 twists have order at most 2 and the device is built on O(2)-orbits, so it is rotation-aware by construction. Theorem R (W3_CLAMP_REDUCTION.md) is itself a base-topology statement: clamp realizability is a question about a bi-concyclic subvariety of R(H-w). So the missing chi-6 certificate should be a HOLONOMY DEFECT: a closed rotational chain of frames whose geometric twist has no fixed 5-coloring chain, i.e., the route-(ii) imprimitivity that provably cannot come from local ports, supplied globally by monodromy.

conjectures.name: C1: Kinematic Carousel Principle (chi >= 6 as twisted-transfer holonomy defect)
conjectures.statement: There exists a finite chi=5 UDG core X with an embedding p having exact rotational symmetry rho of order m about a center O (inducing twist sigma in Aut(X)), an integer N >= 2, and angles 0 = theta_0 < theta_1 < ... < theta_N = 2pi/m (each algebraic, chosen so that many cross-frame pairs (x,y) in X x X satisfy |rho_{theta_{i+1}} x - rho_{theta_i} y| = 1 exactly), such that the finite union U = union of frames X_i = rho_{theta_i}(p(X)) with ALL unit-distance edges satisfies chi(U) >= 6. Moreover the UNSAT certificate factors as twisted-transfer fixed-point emptiness: writing R_i subset Col_5(X) x Col_5(X) for the joint-extendability relation across the i-th cross-frame edge set, there is no coloring c with (c, c compose sigma^{-1}) in R_{N-1} compose ... compose R_0. The closure twist is geometric, not assumed: frame N IS frame 0 as a point set, relabeled by sigma. The Moser spindle is exactly the k=3 instance (2-frame carousel of diamonds about a shared hub, relation I, twist by the hub rotation), so the principle has a verified instance one chromatic level down.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Pipeline runnable in-repo: take a symmetric chi-5 core (from C2's spectroscopy, or a Parts-style symmetric graph), enumerate exact angles theta solving |rho_theta x - y| = 1 over orbit pairs (sympy, number field), rank angles by cross-frame coincidence count, assemble U for N = 2..6, SAT-test 5-colorability (cadical). Concrete falsifier of the mechanism (cheap, runs first): compute the relations R_i for the top-coincidence angles and check whether the composite relation strictly shrinks as frames are chained. If for EVERY realizable angle the relation R_i is the full relation J on Col_5(X)/blocks (no shrinkage), the carousel principle is dead and should be logged as a kill.
conjectures.why_plausible: Every chromatic jump ever achieved in the plane used rotated-copy coincidence (spindle at arccos(5/6)-type angles; de Grey's N from rotated L-copies), but the lineage's UNSAT mechanism was local counting (mono-triples), and L55-L57 prove local mechanisms are exhausted at k=5. If chi = 6 holds via a finite graph, the certificate MUST have a global shape compatible with the monoid obstruction; a closed twisted loop of wide interfaces is the minimal such shape, and the twist by a fixed-point-free relabeling is the canonical imprimitive ingredient that L55 route (ii) demands. The fuel (symmetric chi-5 cores near the UNSAT edge) exists in quantity.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: chi(R^2) may be 5 (de Grey's omega<=3 rival hypothesis), making all such U 5-colorable forever. Or realizable cross-frame edge sets may only ever induce relations too weak to shrink the composite (coloring entropy beats the twist; L55 warned lambda_1 grows). Or every symmetric chi-5 core may have so many colorings that the twisted relation always has a fixed chain for all realizable angle sequences.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Rotated-copy constructions are standard (de Grey 2018, Exoo-Ismailescu rings, Parts' symmetric graphs), but the design target 'twisted transfer fixed-point emptiness around a CLOSED rotational loop, with the twist supplied by the embedding symmetry and the imprimitivity supplied by holonomy rather than gadget combinatorics' has to my knowledge never been stated. Nearest prior art: de Grey's rotated-L assembly (open chain, counting-based UNSAT) and this repo's L55 transfer-matrix formalism (linear chains, untwisted).

conjectures.name: C2: Symmetric-sector forcing in the lineage (twist spectroscopy)
conjectures.statement: At least one of the 12 lineage chi-5 UDGs (S199, L403, 510, 517, 529, 553, 610, 633, T721, 803, 826, 874) admits an exact geometric symmetry sigma of its embedded vertex set (rotation or reflection) such that NO proper 5-coloring c satisfies c = c compose sigma, while no sigma-orbit contains an adjacent pair (so the emptiness is not adjacency-trivial). This is a NONLOCAL forcing phenomenon disjoint from L56/L57: those swept pairwise relations c(s) = c(t) and found all ~2.29M pairs free; sigma-invariance imposes ~|V|(1 - 1/ord(sigma)) simultaneous equalities, a sector L57 never tested. Any nontrivial UNSAT here is simultaneously (a) the first global 5-color forcing in the lineage, (b) a certified carousel core for C1, and (c) a literal imprimitivity certificate for L55 route (ii): the symmetric block of the transfer structure is empty.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: One script, one run: for each lineage graph, compute the exact geometric symmetry group from its .vtx coordinates (candidate isometries from centroid + same-radius vertex images, certified in exact arithmetic), then for each non-identity sigma without orbit-internal adjacency, merge color variables along sigma-orbits and run cadical at k=5. C2 is TRUE iff some (graph, sigma) returns UNSAT nontrivially; FALSE iff every such instance returns an invariant 5-coloring. Either outcome is logged: all-SAT kills lineage cores for the carousel and outputs the exact catalog of (sigma, invariant coloring) fixed points that purpose-built cores must destroy.
conjectures.why_plausible: Lineage graphs are assembled from rotated copies, so they carry rich exact symmetries; an invariant 5-coloring is a 5-coloring of the quotient pseudograph, whose effective constraint density is ord(sigma) times higher; the lineage sits at the 5-colorability edge, so quotients plausibly tip over. The analogous tipping is exactly how the spindle works at k=3 (the rotated diamond admits no invariant-compatible chain).
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Parts and Heule explicitly exploited symmetric colorings as search accelerators and often FOUND them; near-critical graphs may stay 5-colorable in every symmetric sector because builders implicitly selected for that. Also large-order symmetries may all have orbit-internal adjacencies (only trivial UNSAT), leaving no nontrivial instances at all.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Jaan Parts (Geombinatorics, Polymath16 threads) used symmetry-restricted colorings as a heuristic to FIND colorings faster; the systematic emptiness question across the whole lineage at k=5, with the trivial/nontrivial dichotomy via orbit-internal adjacency and the interpretation of nontrivial emptiness as a transfer-imprimitivity certificate, appears unstated anywhere.

conjectures.name: C3: No concyclicity-clamp rigidity, and W3 collapses to pure variety-nonemptiness
conjectures.statement: For a K4-free 6-critical H with split vertex w (A disjoint-union B = N_H(w), |A|,|B| >= 2), let V_{H,w,A,B} subset R(H-w) be the bi-concyclic variety: unit realizations q of H-w with A on a radius-1 circle (center s*(q)) and B on a radius-1 circle (center t*(q)). Part (i) (lemma-level, for VERIFIER): ANY point of V certifies chi(R^2) >= 6, including diagonal points s* = t*: a diagonal point gives a unit map of H itself, and coincidence of nonadjacent vertices is harmless for lower bounds (a plane 5-coloring restricted to the point set still properly 5-colors H). Hence Theorem R's side condition s* != t* is NOT load-bearing for the bound, and W3 reduces to bare real-variety nonemptiness over the recursively enumerable family of (H,w,A,B). Part (ii) (the conjecture): 'concyclicity-clamp rigidity' is FALSE, i.e., it is not the case that V is empty for every K4-free 6-critical H; some H assembled from carousel geometry (C1) has nonempty V, and on any positive-dimensional component the center-separation f = |s* - t*|^2 is non-constant real-analytic, so realizations with separated centers exist in abundance once V is nonempty at all.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Two-stage numeric probe with scipy.optimize.least_squares (no Groebner, respecting the 14-vertex exact wall): (1) the repo's 48-vtx triangle-free witness H, system in 2*47 + 4 unknowns with 213 edge equations + 23 concyclicity equations, 1000 random multi-starts; PREDICTION: residual floor stays above 1e-3 (this abstract H is not geometric; failure is the expected, informative outcome and quantifies WHERE the obstruction concentrates via per-equation residuals). (2) Same probe on carousel-derived H from C1/C2 outputs; C3 predicts some such H reaches residual < 1e-12 with f > 0.01, after which exact certification of the small angle subsystem is attempted in sympy. Part (i) is a finite check VERIFIER can formalize now against Theorem R's proof in W3_CLAMP_REDUCTION.md.
conjectures.why_plausible: Pinning s* = t* is 2 extra real conditions on top of bi-concyclicity, so the diagonal is codimension 2 inside V wherever V is positive-dimensional; trapping f at 0 on a whole component would be a strong new rigidity theorem with no known mechanism (triangle-free H-w has no rigid triangles, so its realization spaces are typically flexible). L52 already showed naive over-determination counts do not decide realizability (P_510 realizes at +1487), so the overdetermined look of the system is not evidence of emptiness.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: If chi(R^2) = 5 then V is empty for EVERY K4-free 6-critical H (by part (i) the bound would follow otherwise), which would make emptiness a sweeping rigidity fact and de Grey's omega<=3 intuition the deep truth. Independently, part (ii)'s analytic claim could fail if some component of V is itself rigid (0-dimensional) and happens to sit on the diagonal.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Theorem R is this repo's own result; the strengthening in part (i) (diagonal points also certify chi >= 6, so the s* != t* condition can be discarded for the bound and W3 becomes pure nonemptiness) appears in neither W3_CLAMP_REDUCTION.md nor any literature I know. Nearest prior art: Theorem R itself, plus combinatorial rigidity of frameworks (Connelly flexes, Hochberg-O'Donnell flexible UDG tricks), never combined with chromatic forcing.

experiment.description: Twist spectroscopy of the lineage (decides C2, gates C1). New script C:\Users\Owen\dev\hadwiger-nelson\experiments\combinatorial\twist_spectroscopy.py: (1) load each of the 12 lineage graphs via the existing f1pt_lib parse_vtx/parse_edges (VTX/.vtx, EDGE/.edge, same loader as shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py); (2) compute the exact geometric symmetry group of each embedding: centroid, then candidate isometries generated by mapping one max-radius reference vertex to each same-radius vertex with both orientations, certified by exact (sympy) vertex-set permutation check, with 60-digit mpmath screening first for speed; (3) for each non-identity symmetry sigma, classify orbits: if any orbit contains an adjacent pair, record 'trivial UNSAT' without solving; otherwise build the k=5 CNF with color variables merged along sigma-orbits and solve with Cadical195; (4) cache per-(graph, sigma) results under experiments/combinatorial/_cache/twist_spectroscopy/: invariant coloring witness (SAT), or the headline nontrivial UNSAT. Decision rule: any nontrivial UNSAT proves C2, hands C1 a certified carousel core with explicit twist, and gives the first symmetric-sector imprimitivity certificate for L55 route (ii); all-SAT falsifies C2 on the lineage and outputs the complete fixed-point catalog (sigma, invariant colorings) that purpose-built carousel cores must be designed to destroy. Either branch is a publishable learning (L58 candidate) and strictly narrows where the missing object lives.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Everything needed exists: .vtx/.edge files and loaders (f1pt_lib), python-sat Cadical195 (used in shotD), sympy + mpmath for exact symmetry certification, no nauty needed (geometric symmetries, not abstract automorphisms), no C compiler needed. Quotient CNFs are SMALLER than the routine full 5-coloring instances already solved on these graphs.
experiment.est_cost: ~150 lines of Python; symmetry detection seconds-to-minutes per graph; each quotient SAT solve seconds-to-minutes; full 12-graph sweep well under 2 hours on this host.

detector_self_check.Q2: The mechanism consumes objects that do not exist over Q^2: chi-5 cores contain unit equilateral triangles, and Q^2's unit-distance graph is triangle-free with chi = 2 (Woodall), so no carousel core embeds. Deeper, the monodromy sources themselves vanish: Q^2-points of realization varieties are totally disconnected (no flexes, no IVT, no wall-crossing), and the coincidence-tuning angles are arccos-of-algebraic-irrational values with no rational-point realizations. Nothing in C1-C3 lifts to Q^2, so the argument cannot over-prove chi(Q^2) >= 3; the Euclidean content enters exactly through completeness of R and the algebraic angles.
detector_self_check.Linf: Three independent failures in (R^2, L^infty), each consumed essentially by the construction: (1) fuel: the carousel needs a finite chi-5 core, and no such core embeds where chi = 4 (Chilakamarri); (2) tuning: the linear isometry group is the order-8 dihedral group, no SO(2) continuum, so cross-frame unit coincidences cannot be dialed in and order-m twists for m > 4 do not exist; (3) rigidity: L^infty unit circles intersect in segments, so Theorem R's center-pinning (centers determined once |A| >= 3) and the strict-convexity rigidity behind isostatic frameworks both fail; the bi-concyclic variety degenerates to positive-dimensional center families and f loses meaning. The argument is not valid in a general normed plane.
detector_self_check.R1: The device is built ON the rotation group: twists sigma come from order-m rotational embedding symmetries and frames are SO(2)-orbits, so it is rotation-aware by construction, not rotation-blind. On R^1 the isometry twist group is {+-1} (order-m monodromy for m >= 3 impossible), the unit-distance graph has chi = 2 with no chi-5 cores, and realization 'varieties' on the line are discrete sign choices with no flexes. The mechanism is inert on R^1 and constrains nothing there.
